feedback.text: Service providers offer the services which are essential for the continuity of the large mass of online services and should have no authority over the type of customers they offer this service. These include in particular the few payment service providers that dominate the market (visa, mastercard, Paypal) but also large hosting providers such as AWS and Google Cloud. If these providers have the same/similar directive which services they do not want to support, this amounts to de facto censorship of the market as a whole. Essential service providers should have to do everything that is legal by law.
